questions.question: Why was the 2023-2024 era of multi-agent evaluation "chaos"?
options: There were no multi-agent systems to evaluate
Everyone picked their own metrics, baselines, and task sets — claims of "better" could not be compared, and without hold-outs, training contamination inflated scores
Benchmarks were too expensive to run
Evaluation was illegal in some jurisdictions
questions.explanation: The 2025-2026 benchmark wave (MARBLE, COMMA, MedAgentBoard, AgentArch, SWE-bench Pro) imposed structure. The skeptical reading skill — better than what, on what, measured how — is the lesson's core.

questions.question: What uncomfortable result did COMMA report?
options: All models solved it perfectly
Frontier models including GPT-4o struggle to beat a RANDOM baseline on multimodal asymmetric-information coordination — multi-modality agent cooperation is under-trained and under-evaluated
The benchmark could not be reproduced
Only open-weights models passed
questions.explanation: Agents with different observation modalities coordinating without full information sharing is where collaboration collapses. The null result is a warning: measure before claiming multimodal coordination works.

questions.question: What did MedAgentBoard conclude about multi-agent vs single-LLM?
options: Multi-agent dominates all medical tasks

Multi-agent does NOT dominate on most categories — decomposition helps when subtasks separate cleanly (diagnosis + treatment) and hurts when coordination overhead exceeds specialization gain (report generation)
Single LLMs are unsafe for medicine
Rule-based systems beat both everywhere
questions.explanation: If this lesson generalizes, many proposed multi-agent systems are over-engineered. The benchmark's role: force a credible single-LLM baseline before paying the coordination tax.

questions.question: What does the SWE-bench Verified vs Pro gap (~70%+ vs ~23% raw) signal?
options: Pro's grader is broken
Contamination — Verified leaked into training corpora by mid-2025, so passing it is no longer evidence of capability; Pro (1,865 problems, 41 repos, later cutoffs) is the gating reality check
Pro tasks are in different languages
Verified is harder than Pro
questions.explanation: The same models that ace Verified score ~23-35% raw on Pro. Notably, agent-team scaffolding produces a ~30-40 point delta on Pro — one of 2026's strongest empirical arguments FOR multi-agent coordination.

questions.question: What does MARBLE's milestone-KPI design add over pass/fail scoring?
options: Faster benchmark runs

Partial-progress tracking — coordination differences show up in how far systems get, not just whether they finish; it also quantified the graph-topology research win and the ~3% gain from cognitive planning
Automatic prompt optimization
Multi-language support
questions.explanation: Binary success hides where coordination breaks down. Milestones expose it — which is how MARBLE measured topology effects and the ~4-agent coordination tax apples-to-apples.
